text.title: RTO/RPO Measurement Template Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every DR-drill measurement run must enforce so the resulting RTO/RPO numbers are actual observations, not vibes.

rule.statement: The drill log must capture eight typed checkpoints with timestamps: t0_drill_start, t1_failure_injected, t2_detection_recognised, t3_decision_to_failover, t4_failover_begin, t5_primary_traffic_restored, t6_data_loss_window_measured, t7_drill_closed. A drill with a missing checkpoint is recorded as `measurement: partial` and explicitly disqualified from trending.
rule.rationale: RTO and RPO are computed deltas (RTO = t5-t1, RPO = t6 data-window). Missing any of the eight makes the math impossible. Naming the partial state instead of fudging missing data preserves the integrity of the multi-drill trend.

rule.statement: All eight checkpoints must be timestamped against a single declared authoritative clock (named NTP server URL or one operator's monotonic clock). Mixing clocks across checkpoints is forbidden; the clock identity is recorded once at the top of the log.
rule.rationale: Wall-clock skew across operator laptops produces minute-level errors that quietly inflate or deflate measured RTO. The single-clock rule removes a class of measurement noise that is otherwise nearly impossible to audit after the fact.

rule.statement: Each checkpoint timestamp must be confirmed by an observer who is not on the failover-execution team. Observer confirms by countersignature on the log (handle + time-of-countersign within 5 minutes of the checkpoint).
rule.rationale: Operators under pressure round timestamps subconsciously — they want the drill to look good. An independent observer turns the timestamp from a self-report into a witnessed observation. The 5-minute countersign window prevents reconstruction after the drill ends.

rule.statement: RPO measurement (t6_data_loss_window_measured) must be expressed as a closed interval [earliest_lost_write_ts, latest_lost_write_ts] derived from a query against the recovered system, not as a guess. If no write loss occurred, the field is `data_loss_window: empty (verified-by-query)`.
rule.rationale: "We probably lost about 5 minutes" is not an RPO measurement. The closed-interval requirement forces an actual query against the recovered data (e.g. max-id in primary vs recovered) and converts RPO from estimation into observation.

rule.statement: Within 1 business day of drill close, the conductor produces a reconciliation record carrying: measured_rto, measured_rpo, target_rto, target_rpo, deltas, root-cause notes for any delta >25%, and a `verified-by: <observer_handle>` field. The reconciliation is what feeds the tracking board — the raw log alone does not.
rule.rationale: Raw timestamps without reconciliation rot in the drill folder. The 1-business-day window keeps the reconciliation cheap (memory still fresh) while still forcing a structured second-pass that catches obvious data-entry errors in the live log.

rule.statement: The reconciliation must include a signed delta (measured − target) for both RTO and RPO. Any positive delta >25% of target, or two consecutive drills with same-sign delta, triggers a target review or remediation plan — the team is not allowed to close the loop with "we'll do better next time".
rule.rationale: Drills become theatre when "almost made it" is the standing answer. A typed escalation threshold converts a soft observation into an obligation to either fix the system or renegotiate the target with the rpo-rto-negotiation-guide.

example.description: Closed measurement record for a partial failover drill on a payments DB.
example.code: record_id: drill-2026-05-20-payments-failover clock: ntp://time.cloudflare.com conductor: @sre-lead observer: @secops-onduty checkpoints: t0_drill_start: 2026-05-20T10:00:00Z (obs-countersigned @10:01) t1_failure_injected: 2026-05-20T10:05:00Z (obs-countersigned @10:05) t2_detection_recognised: 2026-05-20T10:09:14Z t3_decision_to_failover: 2026-05-20T10:12:00Z t4_failover_begin: 2026-05-20T10:12:35Z t5_primary_traffic_restored:2026-05-20T10:24:48Z t6_data_loss_window: empty (verified-by-query select max(id) cross-check) t7_drill_closed: 2026-05-20T10:35:00Z reconciliation: measured_rto: 19m48s (target: 1h) delta: -40m12s OK measured_rpo: 0 (target: 15m) delta: -15m OK root_cause_notes: none verified_by: @secops-onduty
